evals.prompt: Run /validate-env to check if this project is set up correctly
evals.expected_output: A formatted health check report with PASS/FAIL/WARN for each category: Configuration, GitHub, Git Branches, Docs, Labels, CLAUDE.md, Security
expectations: Output includes a section header for 'Configuration' checks
Output includes a section header for 'GitHub' connection check using gh auth status
Output includes a section header for 'Git Branches' checks
Output includes a section header for 'Docs' checks
Output reports PASS, FAIL, or WARN for each check
Output includes a final summary with total PASS/FAIL/WARN counts
Output provides actionable remediation for any FAIL or WARN items
Output includes label-presence check for 8 canonical labels
Output includes branch-protection presence check for main and develop

evals.prompt: Run /validate-env on a project that has no .env.claude file
evals.expected_output: Immediate FAIL with clear instructions to create .env.claude from template
expectations: Output suggests creating from template: cp templates/.env.claude.example .env.claude
The command does not crash or produce an unformatted error
